channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canadian-Palestinian Artist Samar Hejazi Showcased at the 2026 Met Gala
item.description: Samar Hejazi, a Canadian-Palestinian multidisciplinary artist, will be prominently featured in this year’s Met Gala at New York’s Metropolitan Museum of Art. Known for her intricate textile work, embr...

item.title: Japan Deploys Firefighters to Control Deadly Wildfires in Northern Region
item.description: Japan has deployed 1,400 firefighters and 100 Self-Defence Force personnel to tackle devastating wildfires that have been raging in the northern region for several days. The fires, now on their fifth ...

item.title: Floods in Mombasa Highlight Weak Infrastructure and Leadership Challenges in Nyali
item.description: Heavy rains on Kenya's coast have caused significant flooding in Nyali, Mombasa, exposing the area's infrastructure weaknesses and sparking public debate over leadership as the 2027 elections approach...

item.title: Discovery of Blood Vessels in T. rex Bones Provides New Insight into Dinosaur Biology
item.description: Scientists have made a groundbreaking discovery in the study of Tyrannosaurus rex fossils, revealing preserved blood vessels within a rib of a T. rex known as 'Scotty,' the largest specimen ever found...

item.title: Congress Supporter Killed in Asansol After Argument, Allegations of Political Link to TMC
item.description: A 43-year-old share trader, Debdeep Chatterjee, was beaten to death by a mob in Asansol on Saturday following an argument when his car brushed against a parked motorbike near his home. The incident oc...

item.title: Tuareg Rebels Resume Fighting in Kidal Against Malian Army and Russian Forces
item.description: Fighting has reignited in Kidal, a key northern city in Mali, with clashes between Tuareg rebels and the Malian army, which is supported by Russian mercenaries. The Azawad Liberation Front (FLA) rebel...

item.title: Gunman Apprehended at White House Correspondents’ Dinner; Secret Service Responds
item.description: During the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington, an attempted attack occurred when a man named Cole Tomas Allen charged a security checkpoint, firing a weapon. A Secret Service officer was...

item.title: Fubara Urges Continuity in Tinubu Administration to Consolidate Progress
item.description: Rivers State Governor, Siminalayi Fubara, don show strong support for President Bola Tinubu re-election, talk say Nigeria dey on correct track and e need steady backing to carry forward ongoing reform...

item.title: Timeline of Events Surrounding the Incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: The White House Correspondents' Dinner, an annual event where journalists and political figures gather, became the scene of a shocking event when a shooting unfolded. The situation began to unravel as...

item.title: Suspect Arrested After Trump Shooting Incident, To Be Charged On Monday
item.description: A suspect has been arrested after a shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington, where US President Donald Trump was present. The suspect, identified as 31-year-old Cole ...

item.title: Key Details About Australia’s 2026 Federal Budget
item.description: Australia’s 2026 federal budget will be handed down on May 12 amid a perfect storm of global energy shocks, rising costs of living, and a mounting national debt nearing $1 trillion. Federal Treasurer ...

item.title: Nairobi Man Leaves Chang’aa Business to Start a Better Life
item.description: In the streets of Githurai 44, Salim, once involved in selling chang’aa, has turned his life around. For many years, he struggled in the illicit alcohol trade, which contributed to addiction and hards...

item.title: Jamaican artiste Goddess Sifaya relocates to Nairobi to reconnect with African heritage
item.description: Yanique McPherson, popularly known as Goddess Sifaya, is a Jamaican singer who has relocated from the Caribbean to Kenya as part of a personal journey to reconnect with her African roots. According to...

item.title: Francine Diaz shares excitement and pressure in first romantic comedy film
item.description: Francine Diaz opened up about her experience starring in her first romantic comedy film titled She Who Must Not Be Named, describing the opportunity as both exciting and challenging. The young actress...

item.title: SERAP and Nigerian Editors Challenge NBC Broadcast Rules at Court Over Press Freedom Concerns
item.description: Di Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project (SERAP) together with di Nigerian Guild of Editors don drag di National Broadcasting Commission (NBC) go court over di directive wey dem call 'arbit...

item.title: Starmer maintains Labour support amid Mandelson appointment controversy
item.description: Keir Starmer continues to assert his leadership of the Labour party despite mounting pressure over his decision to appoint Peter Mandelson as ambassador to Washington. Although several calls for his r...

item.title: Lacson prepares progress report on flood control probe, considers submission to Ombudsman
item.description: Senator Panfilo “Ping” Lacson, chairperson of the Senate Blue Ribbon Committee, announced that he is drafting a chairman’s progress report regarding the ongoing investigation into alleged irregulariti...

item.title: What’s Next for Apple’s Hardware Strategy Under John Ternus?
item.description: Apple’s future hardware strategy is set to shift under new leadership, as John Ternus, a longtime hardware executive, is poised to take over as CEO later this year, succeeding Tim Cook. Ternus, who jo...

item.title: Renowned Indian Photographer Raghu Rai Passes Away at 83
item.description: Raghu Rai, one of India's most celebrated photographers, has passed away in Delhi at the age of 83 after a two-year battle with cancer. Rai, renowned for documenting pivotal historical moments and the...

item.title: China reaffirms political and security support for Myanmar amid regional tensions
item.description: China’s Foreign Minister Wang Yi has reaffirmed Beijing’s strong backing for Myanmar’s sovereignty and security during a high-level meeting with the country’s leader, Min Aung Hlaing, in Naypyidaw. Th...
